Name: Deoxys Normal Forme
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your deck for any Deoxys and switch it with Deoxys Normal Forme. (Any cards attached to Deoxys Normal Forme, damage counters, Special Conditions, and effects on it are now on the new Pokémon.) If you do, put Deoxys Normal Forme on top of your deck. Shuffle your deck afterward. You can’t use more than 1 Form Change Poké-Power each turn.
Effects: Does 20 damage plus 10 more damage for each Energy attached to all of your opponent’s Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: An alien virus that fell to earth on a meteor underwent a DNA mutation to become this Pokémon.

Name: Dragonite
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon.
Flip a coin for each of your opponent’s Pokémon. If that coin flip is heads, this attack does 50 damage to that Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It is said to make its home somewhere in the sea. It guides crews of shipwrecks to shore.

Name: Froslass
Effects: Discard a [P] Energy attached to Froslass. During your opponent’s next turn, if Froslass would be Knocked Out by damage from an attack, the Attacking Pokémon is Knocked Out.
The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep. Put 1 damage counter on each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It freezes foes with an icy breath nearly -60 degrees F. What seems to be its body is actually hollow.

Name: Giratina
Effects: Choose 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. This attack does 20 damage to that Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of an attack, including damage, done to Giratina during your opponent’s next turn.
Does 50 damage plus 10 more damage for each damage counter on the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: A Pokémon that is said to live in a world on the reverse side of ours. It appears in an ancient cemetery.

Name: Gliscor
Effects: Choose either Burned or Poisoned. The Defending Pokémon is now affected by that Special Condition. You may return Gliscor and all cards attached to it to your hand.
If the Defending Pokémon is affected by a Special Condition, this attack does 40 damage plus 40 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It observes prey while hanging inverted from branches. When the chance presents itself, it swoops!

Name: Heatran
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may move a [R] Energy attached to 1 of your Pokémon to Heatran. This power can’t be used if Heatran is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: You may discard as many basic Energy cards as you like attached to Heatran. If you do, this attack does 40 damage plus 20 more damage for each Energy card you discarded.
Flavor-Text: It dwells in volcanic caves. It digs in with its cross-shaped feet to crawl on ceilings and walls.

Name: Kingdra
Effects: Search your discard pile for as many [W] Energy cards as you like, show them to your opponent, and this attack does 10 damage for each [W] Energy card you chose. Put those cards on top of your deck. Shuffle your deck afterward.

You may discard 2 cards from your hand. If you do, this attack does 40 damage plus 20 more damage and does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It lives in caves on the seafloor and creates giant whirlpools every time it moves.

Name: Luxray
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: If your opponent has any Pokémon LV.X in play, each of Luxray’s attacks does 50 more damage to the Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: Search your discard pile for a [L] Energy card and attach it to Luxray.
Discard all [L] Energy attached to Luxray.
Flavor-Text: It has eyes that can see through anything. It spots and captures prey hiding behind objects.

Name: Mamoswine
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed and discard an Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon.

You may do 60 damage plus 40 more damage and 40 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) If you do, Mamoswine does 40 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: Its impressive tusks are made of ice. The population thinned when it turned warm after the ice age.

Name: Metagross
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may flip a coin. If heads, switch 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon with 1 of the Defending Pokémon. This power can’t be used if Metagross is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: During your next turn, Metagross’s Extra Comet Punch attack’s base damage is 100.
Flavor-Text: Metang combined to form it. With four brains, it has the intelligence of a supercomputer.

Name: Mewtwo
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.
Does 40 damage plus 10 more damage for each Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: A Pokémon created by recombining Mew’s genes. It’s said to have the most savage heart among Pokémon.

Name: Politoed
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: If you have Poliwag, Poliwhirl, and Poliwrath in play, each of these Pokémon’s attacks does 60 more damage to the Defending Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 damage plus 40 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It gathers groups of others as their leader. Its cries make Poliwag obey.

Name: Probopass
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: Any damage done to Probopass by your opponent’s attacks is reduced by 10 for each [M] Energy attached to Probopass (after applying Weakness and Resistance). You can’t reduce more than 20 damage in this way.
Effects: Choose a number of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon up to the amount of [M] Energy attached to Probopass. This attack does 20 damage to each of them. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It exudes strong magnetism from all over. It controls three small units call Mini-Noses.

Name: Rayquaza
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may flip a coin until you get tails. For each heads, search your discard pile for a basic [R] Energy card or a basic [L] Energy card and attach it to Rayquaza. This power can’t be used if Rayquaza is affected by a Special Condition or if you have another Rayquaza in play.
Effects: Discard all Energy attached to Rayquaza.
Flavor-Text: It lives in the ozone layer far above the clouds and cannot be seen from the ground.

Name: Regigigas
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: Regigigas can’t attack until your opponent has 3 or less Prize cards left.
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack’s base damage is 40 instead of 120.
Flavor-Text: There is an enduring legend that states this Pokémon towed continents with ropes.

Name: Spiritomb
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn, when you put Spiritomb from your hand onto your Bench, you may put 1 damage counter on all Pokémon that already have any damage counters on them (both yours and your opponent’s). You can’t use more than 1 Curse Breath Poké-Power each turn.
Effects: Move 1 damage counter from 1 of your Pokémon to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: A Pokémon that was formed by 108 spirits. It is bound to a fissure in an odd keystone.

Name: Yanmega
Effects: Does 10 damage to each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.
If the Defending Pokémon already has 2 or more damage counters on it, this attack does 60 damage plus 20 more damage and switch Yanmega with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: By churning its wings, it creates shock waves that inflict critical internal injuries to foes.

Name: Armaldo
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: If Armaldo would be damaged by an attack, prevent that attack’s damage done to Armaldo if that damage is 60 or less.
Effects: During your next turn, if an attack does damage to the Defending Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance), that attack does 40 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It went ashore after evolving. Its entire body is clad in a sturdy armor.

Name: Azelf
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn, when you put Azelf from your hand onto your Bench, you may look at all of your face-down Prize cards. If you do, you may choose 1 Pokémon you find there, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Then, choose 1 card in your hand and put it as a Prize card face down.
Effects: The Defending Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: Known as “The Being of Willpower.” It sleeps at the bottom of a lake to keep the world in balance.

Name: Bellossom
Effects: Remove 1 damage counter from each of your Pokémon.
Does 40 damage plus 20 more damage for each Vileplume and each Bellossom you have in play. Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Burned.
Flavor-Text: When the heavy rainfall season ends, it is drawn out by warm sunlight to dance in the open.

Name: Cradily
Effects: Before doing damage, you may switch 1 of the Defending Pokémon with 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. If you do, this attack does 30 damage to the new Defending Pokémon. If the Defending Pokémon would be Knocked Out by this attack, you may remove all damage counters from Cradily.
The Defending Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: It lives in the shallows of warm seas. When the tide goes out, it digs up prey from beaches.

Name: Crawdaunt
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent returns the Defending Pokémon and all cards attached to it to his or her hand.
If Crawdaunt has a Technical Machine card attached to it, this attack does 50 damage plus 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It is a ruffian that uses its pincers to pick up and toss out other Pokémon from its pond.

Name: Delcatty
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: If Delcatty is your Active Pokémon and is damaged by an opponent’s attack (even if Delcatty is Knocked Out), flip a coin. If heads, the Attacking Pokémon is now Confused.
Effects: Search your discard pile for a basic Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It is highly popular among female Trainers for its sublime fur. It does not keep a nest.

Name: Deoxys Attack Forme
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your deck for any Deoxys and switch it with Deoxys Attack Forme. (Any cards attached to Deoxys Attack Forme, damage counters, Special Conditions, and effects on it are now on the new Pokémon.) If you do, put Deoxys Attack Forme on top of your deck. Shuffle your deck afterward. You can’t use more than 1 Form Change Poké-Power each turn.
Effects: During your next turn, Deoxys’s Psychic Boost attack’s base damage is 20.
Flavor-Text: An alien virus that fell to earth on a meteor underwent a DNA mutation to become this Pokémon.

Name: Deoxys Defense Forme
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your deck for any Deoxys and switch it with Deoxys Defense Forme. (Any cards attached to Deoxys Defense Forme, damage counters, Special Conditions, and effects on it are now on the new Pokémon.) If you do, put Deoxys Defense Forme on top of your deck. Shuffle your deck afterward. You can’t use more than 1 Form Change Poké-Power each turn.
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, prevent all effects of an attack, and any damage done to Deoxys by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: An alien virus that fell to earth on a meteor underwent a DNA mutation to become this Pokémon.

Name: Deoxys Speed Forme
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your deck for any Deoxys and switch it with Deoxys Speed Forme. (Any cards attached to Deoxys Speed Forme, damage counters, Special Conditions, and effects on it are now on the new Pokémon.) If you do, put Deoxys Speed Forme on top of your deck. Shuffle your deck afterward. You can’t use more than 1 Form Change Poké-Power each turn.
Effects: Choose 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage to that Pokémon. This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Weakness, Resistance, Poké-Powers, Poké-Bodies, or any other effects on that Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: An alien virus that fell to earth on a meteor underwent a DNA mutation to become this Pokémon.

Name: Ditto
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: As long as Ditto is your Active Pokémon, its maximum HP is the same as your opponent’s Active Pokémon. Ditto can use the attacks of that Pokémon as its own. (You still need the necessary Energy to use each attack.) If that Pokémon is no longer your opponent’s Active Pokémon, choose 1 of your opponent’s Active Pokémon for Ditto to copy.
Flavor-Text: It has the ability to reconstitute its entire cellular structure to transform into whatever it sees.

Name: Forretress
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: Whenever you attach a basic Energy card from your hand to Forretress (excluding effects of attacks), you may flip a coin. If tails, put 2 damage counters on each Pokémon (both yours and your opponent’s) (excluding any Forretress).
Effects: Forretress does 40 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It is encased in a steel shell. Its peering eyes are all that can be seen of its mysterious innards.

Name: Groudon
Effects: Choose up to 2 basic [F] Energy cards from your hand and attach them to 1 of your Pokémon.
Discard 2 [F] Energy attached to Groudon and this attack does 10 damage to each of your Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It had been asleep in underground magma ever since it fiercely fought Kyogre long ago.

Name: Heatran
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may move a [M] Energy attached to 1 of your Pokémon to Heatran. This power can’t be used if Heatran is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: Flip a coin for each [M] Energy attached to Heatran. This attack does 40 damage plus 20 more damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: It dwells in volcanic caves. It digs in with its cross-shaped feet to crawl on ceilings and walls.

Name: Jirachi
Effects: Search your deck for any 1 card. Shuffle your deck, then put that card on top of your deck.
Discard all Energy attached to Jirachi. The Defending Pokémon is Knocked Out at the end of your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: It is said to have the ability to grant any wish for just one week every thousand years.

Name: Kyogre
Effects: Choose up to 2 basic [W] Energy cards from your hand and attach them to 1 of your Pokémon.
Discard 2 [W] Energy attached to Kyogre. This attack does 20 damage to each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It is said to have widened the seas by causing downpours. It had been asleep in a marine trench.

Name: Lopunny
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: As long as Lopunny is your Active Pokémon, remove 1 damage counter from each of your Benched Pokémon between turns.
Effects: Draw 3 cards.
If the Defending Pokémon is an Evolved Pokémon, this attack does 30 damage plus 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: An extremely cautious Pokémon. It cloaks its body with its fluffy ear fur when it senses danger.

Name: Mesprit
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn, when you put Mesprit from your hand onto your Bench, you may use this power. Your opponent can’t use any Poké-Powers on his or her Pokémon during your opponent’s next turn.
Effects: If you have the same number of cards in your hand as your opponent, this attack does 20 damage plus 50 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Known as “The Being of Emotion.” It taught humans the nobility of sorrow, pain, and joy.

Name: Poliwrath
Effects: If Poliwrath was damaged by an attack during your opponent’s last turn, this attack does nothing.
Poliwrath does 20 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: With extremely tough muscles, it can keep swimming in the Pacific Ocean without resting.

Name: Regice
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may use this power. Discard 2 cards from your hand and choose 1 of your opponent’s Active Pokémon that isn’t an Evolved Pokémon. Then, your opponent switches that Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon. This power can’t be used if Regice is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: If Regice was damaged by an attack during your opponent’s last turn, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: Its body is made of ice from the ice age. It controls frigid air of -328 degrees Fahrenheit.

Name: Regigigas
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: When you attach an Energy card from your hand to Regigigas, remove all Special Conditions from Regigigas.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 60 damage plus 20 more damage and does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: There is an enduring legend that states this Pokémon towed continents with ropes.

Name: Regirock
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), if you have a [F] Energy card in your discard pile, you may discard 2 cards from your hand. Then, attach a [F] Energy card from your discard pile to Regirock. This power can’t be used if Regirock is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 damage plus 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Its entire body is made of rock. If any part chips off in battle, it attaches rocks to repair itself.

Name: Registeel
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may discard 2 cards from your hand. Then, remove 3 damage counters from Registeel. This power can’t be used if Registeel is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: You may discard an Energy card attached to Registeel. If you do, search your discard pile for an Energy card (excluding the one you discarded) and attach it to Registeel.
Flavor-Text: Tempered by pressure underground over tens of thousands of years, its body cannot be scratched.

Name: Shedinja
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your opponent’s turn, if Shedinja would be Knocked Out by damage from an attack, you may put 4 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon and each of the opponent’s Pokémon that has the same name as the Attacking Pokémon.
Effects: Put 3 damage counters on the Defending Pokémon. If Shedinja has any damage counters on it, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: A discarded bug shell that came to life. Peering into the crack on its back is said to steal one’s spirit.

Name: Torkoal
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: As long as Torkoal is your Active Pokémon, prevent all effects, including damage, done to your Benched Pokémon by your opponent’s attacks.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Burned and Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: It burns coal inside its shell for energy. It blows out black soot if it is endangered.

Name: Unown !
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn, when you put Unown ! from your hand onto your Bench, you may flip a coin. If heads, put 2 damage counters on 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. If tails, put 2 damage counters on 1 of your Pokémon.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage. If tails, Unown ! does 10 damage to itself, and this attack’s damage isn’t affected by Weakness or Resistance.
Flavor-Text: Shaped like an ancient writing, it is a huge mystery whether language or Unown came first.

Name: Uxie
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn, when you put Uxie from your hand onto your Bench, you may draw cards until you have 7 cards in your hand.
Effects: You may put Uxie and all cards attached to it on the bottom of your deck in any order.
Flavor-Text: Known as “The Being of Knowledge.” It is said that it can wipe out the memory of those who see its eyes.

Name: Victreebel
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Burned and Poisoned. Before applying these effects, you may switch 1 of the Defending Pokémon with 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. The new Defending Pokémon is now Burned and Poisoned.
Discard an Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon at the end of your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: It pools in its mouth a fluid with a honeylike scent, which is really an acid that dissolves anything.

Name: Vileplume
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), when you attach a [G] or [P] Energy card from your hand to Vileplume (excluding effects of attacks or Poké-Powers), you may use this power. If you attach a [G] Energy card, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep. If you attach a [P] Energy card, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned. This power can’t be used if Vileplume is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent can’t play any Trainer, Supporter, or Stadium cards from his or her hand during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: Its petals are the largest in the world. As it walks, it scatters extremely allergenic pollen.

Name: Anorith
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, any damage done to Anorith by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 damage plus 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: A Pokémon ancestor that was reanimated from a fossil. It lived in the sea and hunted with claws.

Name: Camerupt
Effects: Each player discards the top card of his or her deck. This attack does 30 damage plus 30 more damage for each Energy card discarded in this way.
Camerupt does 20 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It has volcanoes on its back. If magma builds up in its body, it shudders, then erupts violently.

Name: Castform
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your deck for any Castform and switch it with Castform. (Any cards attached to Castform, damage counters, Special Conditions, and effects on it are now on the new Pokémon.) If you do, put Castform on top of your deck. Shuffle your deck afterward. You can’t use more than 1 Temperament Poké-Power each turn.
Effects: If you have a Stadium card in play, remove 3 damage counters from Castform. If your opponent has a Stadium card in play, this attack does 30 damage plus 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Its appearance changes with the weather. Recently, its molecules were found to be just like water.

Name: Castform Rain Form
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your deck for any Castform and switch it with Castform Rain Form. (Any cards attached to Castform Rain Form, damage counters, Special Conditions, and effects on it are now on the new Pokémon.) Shuffle Castform Rain Form back into your deck. You can’t use more than 1 Temperament Poké-Power each turn.
Effects: The defending Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: Its appearance changes with the weather. Recently, its molecules were found to be just like water.

Name: Castform Snow-Cloud Form
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your deck for any Castform and switch it with Castform Snow-cloud Form. (Any cards attached to Castform Snow-cloud Form, damage counters, Special Conditions, and effects on it are now on the new Pokémon.) Shuffle Castform Snow-cloud Form back into your deck. You can’t use more than 1 Temperament Poké-Power each turn.
Effects: This attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: Its appearance changes with the weather. Recently, its molecules were found to be just like water.

Name: Castform Sunny Form
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your deck for any Castform and switch it with Castform Sunny Form. (Any cards attached to Castform Sunny Form, damage counters, Special Conditions, and effects on it are now on the new Pokémon.) Shuffle Castform Sunny Form back into your deck. You can’t use more than 1 Temperament Poké-Power each turn.
Effects: Discard a [R] Energy attached to Castform Sunny Form.
Flavor-Text: Its appearance changes with the weather. Recently, its molecules were found to be just like water.

Name: Dragonair
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 damage plus 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: If its body takes on an aura, the weather changes instantly. It is said to live in seas and lakes.

Name: Drifblim
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, any damage done to Drifblim by attacks is reduced by 10 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It’s drowzy in daytime, but flies off in the evening in big groups. No one knows where they go.

Name: Exeggutor
Effects: Each player counts the number of cards in his or her opponent’s hand. Each player shuffles his or her hand into his or her deck. Then, each player draws a number of cards up to the number of cards his or her opponent had. (You draw your cards first.)
Discard as many Energy cards as you like attached to your Pokémon. For each Energy card you discarded, flip a coin. This attack does 50 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: It is called “The Walking Jungle.” If a head grows too big, it falls off and becomes an Exeggcute.

Name: Gliscor
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 damage plus 40 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It observes prey while hanging inverted from branches. When the chance presents itself, it swoops!

Name: Grumpig
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Confused. Put 6 damage counters instead of 3 on the Confused Pokémon.
Does 20 damage times the amount of Energy attached to Grumpig.
Flavor-Text: It uses black pearls to amplify its psycho-power. It does an odd dance to gain control over foes.

Name: Houndoom
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: If you have less Benched Pokémon than your opponent, each of Houndoom’s attacks does 40 more damage to the Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, the Defending Pokémon is now Burned.
Flip a coin. If tails, discard 2 [R] Energy attached to Houndoom.
Flavor-Text: Long ago, people imagined its eerie howls to be the call of the grim reaper.

Name: Lanturn
Effects: Move an Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon to another of your opponent’s Pokémon.
The Defending Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: Lanturn’s light can shine up from great depths. It is nicknamed “The Deep-Sea Star.”

Name: Lanturn
Effects: This attack does 30 damage to each of your opponent’s Pokémon that has any Energy cards attached to it. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Does 60 damage plus 10 more damage for each [W] Energy attached to Lanturn.
Flavor-Text: Lanturn’s light can shine up from great depths. It is nicknamed “The Deep-Sea Star.”

Name: Ledian
Effects: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.
You may switch Ledian with 1 of your Benched Pokémon. If you do, move as many Energy cards attached to Ledian as you like to the new Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It uses starlight as energy. When more stars appear at night, the patterns on its back grow larger.

Name: Lucario
Effects: Choose 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage to that Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
If the Defending Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack does 60 damage plus 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It has the ability to sense the auras of all things. It understands human speech.

Name: Luxio
Effects: Does 10 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flip a coin. If tails, discard all [L] Energy attached to Luxio.
Flavor-Text: Its claws loose electricity with enough amperage to cause fainting. They live in small groups.

Name: Marowak
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, Marowak can’t use Heavy Bone during your next turn.
Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 50 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: From its birth, this savage Pokémon constantly holds bones. It is skilled in using them as weapons.

Name: Metang
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of an attack, including damage, done to Metang during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: It is formed by two Beldum joining together. Its steel body won’t be scratched if it collides with a jet.

Name: Metang
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flip 2 coins. This attack does 40 damage plus 20 more damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: It is formed by two Beldum joining together. Its steel body won’t be scratched if it collides with a jet.

Name: Mightyena
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon isn’t an Evolved Pokémon, that Pokémon can’t attack, retreat, or use any Poké-Powers during your opponent’s next turn.
Does 10 damage times the number of Pokémon you have in play.
Flavor-Text: It chases down prey in a pack. It will never disobey the commands of a skilled Trainer.

Name: Ninjask
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn, when you play Ninjask from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon and if your Bench isn’t full, you may put Shedinja onto your Bench as a Basic Pokémon from your hand or your discard pile.
Effects: If your opponent has 6 or more cards in his or her hand, discard a number of cards without looking until your opponent has 5 cards left in his or her hand.
Flavor-Text: Because it moves so quickly, it sometimes becomes unseeable. It congregates around tree sap.

Name: Persian
Effects: Choose 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage to that Pokémon. This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Weakness, Resistance, Poké-Powers, Poké-Bodies, or any other effects on that Pokémon.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 damage plus 10 more damage.
Flavor-Text: A very haughty Pokémon. Among fans, the size of the jewel in its forehead is a topic of much talk.

Name: Piloswine
Effects: You may do 30 damage plus 30 more damage. If you do, Piloswine does 30 damage to itself.
The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: Its shaggy coat makes it unable to see. It checks surroundings with its sensitive nose instead.

Name: Seadra
Effects: You may discard up to 2 [W] Energy cards from your hand. If you do, this attack does 30 damage plus 10 more damage for each Energy card you discarded.
Flavor-Text: Its spines provide protection. Its fins and bones are prized as traditional medicine ingredients.

Name: Starmie
Effects: You may return Starmie and all cards attached to it to your hand.
Choose 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon that has any Poké-Powers or Poké-Bodies. This attack does 50 damage to that Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: At the center of its body is a red core, which sends mysterious radio signals into the night sky.

Name: Swalot
Effects: Before doing damage, count the remaining HP of the Defending Pokémon and Swalot. If the Defending Pokémon has fewer remaining HP than Swalot’s, this attack does 80 damage instead.
The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: It swallows anything whole. It sweats toxic fluids from its follicles to douse foes.

Name: Swellow
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: If Swellow has no Energy attached to it, Swellow’s Retreat Cost is 0.
Effects: If Swellow was on your Bench this turn, this attack’s base damage is 60 instead of 30.
Flavor-Text: It circles the sky in search of prey. When it spots one, it dive steeply to catch the prey.

Name: Tauros
Effects: Flip a coin for each Tauros you have in play. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 damage plus 20 more damage. If tails, Tauros does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: Once it takes aim at its foe, it makes a headlong charge. It is famous for its violent nature.

Name: Tentacruel
Effects: Search your opponent’s discard pile for up to 2 Energy cards and attach them to any of your opponent’s Pokémon in any way you like. For each Energy card attached in this way, this attack does 20 damage to that Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
If Tentacruel has less Energy attached to it than the Defending Pokémon, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed and Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: With 80 tentacles for ensnaring victims, it prevents escape until the prey is weakened by poison.

Name: Unown J
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn, when you put Unown J from your hand onto your Bench, you may flip a coin. If heads, search your discard pile for a Trainer card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand.
Effects: Remove the Special Condition Asleep from the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Shaped like an ancient writing, it is a huge mystery whether language or Unown came first.

Name: Unown R
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn, if Unown R is on your Bench, you may discard Unown R and all cards attached to it. (This doesn’t count as a Knocked Out Pokémon.) Then, draw a card.
Effects: Move any number of basic Energy cards attached to your Pokémon to your other Pokémon in any way you like.
Flavor-Text: Shaped like an ancient writing, it is a huge mystery whether language or Unown came first.

Name: Unown U
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: As long as Unown U is on your Bench, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done by your opponent’s Pokémon to any Unown on your Bench.
Effects: Search your deck for any 1 card and discard it. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flavor-Text: Shaped like an ancient writing, it is a huge mystery whether language or Unown came first.

Name: Unown V
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may remove 2 damage counters from each of your Pokémon. If you do, your turn ends. This power can’t be used if Unown V is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: Does 10 damage to each of your Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Flavor-Text: Shaped like an ancient writing, it is a huge mystery whether language or Unown came first.

Name: Unown W
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: As long as Unown W is your Active Pokémon, any damage done to your Pokémon by an opponent’s attack is reduced by 10 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: Draw a card.
Flavor-Text: Shaped like an ancient writing, it is a huge mystery whether language or Unown came first.

Name: Unown Y
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Unown Y is on your Bench, you may remove 1 damage counter from 1 of your Active Unown and that Unown is now Asleep.
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 Trainer cards, show them to your opponent, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flavor-Text: Shaped like an ancient writing, it is a huge mystery whether language or Unown came first.

Name: Unown ?
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Unown ? is on your Bench, you may choose a Pokémon in your hand and put it face down. Your opponent guesses a type of that Pokémon. Reveal that card. If your opponent guessed wrong, draw a card. Then, put that card back into your hand.
Effects: Discard up to 2 cards from your hand. For each card you discarded, draw a card.
Flavor-Text: Shaped like ancient writing, it is a huge mystery whether language or Unown came first.

Name: Beldum
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), when you attach a [M] Energy card from your hand to Beldum (excluding effects of attacks or Poké-Powers), you may search your deck for Beldum and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward. This power can’t be used if Beldum is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: Beldum does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It converses with others by using magnetic pulses. In a swarm, they move in perfect unison.

Name: Beldum
Effects: Draw a card.
Flavor-Text: It converses with others by using magnetic pulses. In a swarm, they move in perfect unison.

Name: Bellsprout
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon can’t use any Poké-Powers during your opponent’s next turn.
Bellsprout does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It prefers hot and humid environments. It is quick at capturing prey with its vines.

Name: Buneary
Effects: Remove all Special Conditions and 4 damage counters from Buneary. Buneary is now Asleep.
Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: It slams foes by sharply uncoiling its rolled ears. It stings enough to make a grown-up cry in pain.

Name: Chinchou
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: It discharges positive and negative electricity from its antenna tips to shock its foes.

Name: Chinchou
Effects: If Chinchou has any [W] Energy attached to it, this attack does 20 damage plus 10 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It discharges positive and negative electricity from its antenna tips to shock its foes.

Name: Corphish
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, choose 1 card from your opponent’s hand without looking and discard it.
Flavor-Text: Its hardy vitality enables it to adapt to any environment. Its pincers will never release prey.

Name: Cubone
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: When it thinks of its dead mother, it cries. Its crying makes the skull it wears rattle hollowly.

Name: Dratini
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: It is called the “Mirage Pokémon” because so few have seen it. Its shed skin has been found.

Name: Drifloon
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: A Pokémon formed by the spirits of people and Pokémon. It loves damp, humid seasons.

Name: Exeggcute
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 in any combination of [G] Basic Pokémon and [P] Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.
The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: Its six eggs converse using telepathy. They can quickly gather if they become separated.

Name: Gligar
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It sails on the winds with its limbs extended to strike from the sky. It aims for the prey’s face.

Name: Gligar
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: It sails on the winds with its limbs extended to strike from the sky. It aims for the prey’s face.

Name: Gloom
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.
Choose 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon that doesn’t have any damage counters on it. This attack does 30 damage to that Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: The honey it drools from its mouth smells so atrocious, it can curl noses more than a mile away.

Name: Gloom
Effects: Remove 2 damage counters from Gloom. Gloom is now Asleep.
Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused and Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: The honey it drools from its mouth smells so atrocious, it can curl noses more than a mile away.

Name: Gulpin
Effects: Choose 1 of the Defending Pokémon’s attacks. That Pokémon can’t use that attack during your opponent’s next turn.
Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: Almost all its body is its stomach. Its harsh digestive juices quickly dissolve anything it swallows.

Name: Hitmonchan
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, if Hitmonchan is damaged by an opponent’s attack (even if Hitmonchan is Knocked Out), put 4 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.
If Tyrogue is anywhere under Hitmonchan, this attack’s base damage is 60 instead of 30.
Flavor-Text: The arm-twisting punches it throws pulverize even concrete. It rests after three minutes of fighting.

Name: Hitmonlee
Effects: If Tyrogue is anywhere under Hitmonlee, you may do 30 damage to any 1 Benched Pokémon instead. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: Its legs can stretch double. First-time foes are startled by its extensible reach.

Name: Hitmontop
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.
If Tyrogue is anywhere under Hitmontop, you may switch Hitmontop with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It fights while spinning like a top. The centrifugal force boosts its destructive power by ten.

Name: Horsea
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent’s next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.
Switch Horsea with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It makes its nest in the shade of corals. If it senses danger, it spits murky ink and flees.

Name: Houndour
Effects: Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It conveys its feelings using different cries. It works in a pack to cleverly take down prey.

Name: Ledyba
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.
Ledyba does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It is so timid, it can’t move if it isn’t with a swarm of others. It conveys its feelings with scent.

Name: Lileep
Effects: Choose 1 card from your opponent’s hand without looking. Look at that card you chose, then have your opponent shuffle that card into his or her deck.
Remove 2 damage counters from Lileep.
Flavor-Text: It lived on the seafloor 100 million years ago and was reanimated scientifically.

Name: Meowth
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, draw a card.
Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: It is nocturnal in nature. If it spots something shiny, its eyes glitter brightly.

Name: Misdreavus
Effects: Search your deck for a basic Energy card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
If your opponent has only 1 Prize card left, this attack does 10 damage plus 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It loves to sneak up on people late at night, then startle them with its shrieklike cry.

Name: Nincada
Effects: The Defending Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: It grows underground, sensing its surroundings using antennae instead of its virtually blind eyes.

Name: Nosepass
Effects: If Probopass is on your Bench, this attack does 20 damage plus 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Its nose is a magnet. As a result, this Pokémon always keeps its face pointing north.

Name: Numel
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Burned.
Flavor-Text: Its humped back stores intensely hot magma. In rain, the magma cools, slowing its movement.

Name: Oddish
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Remove 1 damage counter from Oddish.
Flavor-Text: It often plants its root feet in the ground during the day and sows seeds as it walks about at night.

Name: Oddish
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: It often plants its root feet in the ground during the day and sows seeds as it walks about at night.

Name: Pineco
Effects: This attack does 10 damage to each Benched Pokémon (both yours and your opponent’s). (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) Pineco does 50 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It looks just like a pinecone. Its shell protects it from bird Pokémon that peck it by mistake.

Name: Poliwag
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused. If tails, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: Its skin is so thin, its internal organs are visible. It has trouble walking on its newly grown feet.

Name: Poliwhirl
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: The spiral pattern on its belly subtly undulates. Staring at it gradually causes drowsiness.

Name: Poochyena
Effects: The Defending Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: A Pokémon with a persistent nature, it chases its chosen prey until the prey becomes exhausted.

Name: Riolu
Flavor-Text: The aura that emanates from its body intensifies to alert others if it is afraid or sad.

Name: Shinx
Effects: Search your deck for a [L] Energy card attach it to Shinx. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: All of its fur dazzles if danger is sensed. It flees while the foe is momentarily blinded.

Name: Skitty
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, any damage done by attacks from the Defending Pokémon is reduced by 20 (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: It can’t stop itself from chasing moving things, and it runs in a circle, chasing its own tail.

Name: Sneasel
Effects: Look at your opponent’s hand. If your opponent has any Pokémon Tool or Technical Machine cards in his or her hand, put those cards on top of his or her deck. Your opponent shuffles his or her deck afterward.
Flavor-Text: It feeds on eggs stolen from nests. Its sharply hooked claws rip vulnerable spots on prey.

Name: Spoink
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Confused.
You may switch Spoink with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It bounces constantly, using its tail like a spring. The shock of bouncing keeps its heart beating.

Name: Staryu
Effects: Discard an Energy attached to Staryu and remove 3 damage counters from Staryu.
Flavor-Text: Its body is virtually composed of water. It shoots strange beams from its crystal-like eyes.

Name: Swinub
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, put any 1 card from your discard pile into your hand.
Flavor-Text: It loves eating mushrooms that grow under dead grass. It also finds hot springs while foraging.

Name: Taillow
Effects: During your next turn, Tailow’s Peck attack’s base damage is 30.
Flavor-Text: It has a gutsy spirit that makes it bravely take on tough foes. It flies in search of warm climates.

Name: Tentacool
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of an attack, including damage, done to Tentacool during your opponent’s next turn.
Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its body is virtually composed of water. It shoots strange beams from its crystal-like eyes.

Name: Tyrogue
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may put Hitmonlee, Hitmonchan, or Hitmontop from your hand onto Tyrogue (this counts as evolving Tyrogue) and remove all damage counters from Tyrogue.
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon is an Evolved Pokémon, this attack does 10 damage plus 20 more damage and Tyrogue does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It is famous for its eagerness to fight and always nurses injuries from challenging larger foes.

Name: Weepinbell
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: A Pokémon that appears to be a plant. It captures unwary prey by dousing them with a toxic powder.

Name: Yanma
Effects: Switch Yanma with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of an attack, including damage, done to Yanma during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: Its eyes can see 360 degrees without moving its head. It won’t miss prey—even those behind it.

Name: Bubble Coat
Description: As long as Bubble Coat is attached to a Pokémon, that Pokémon has no Weakness. If that Pokémon is damaged by an opponent’s attack, discard this card at the end of the turn.
Tool-Rule: Attach Bubble Coat to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it. If that Pokémon is Knocked Out, discard this card.

Name: Buck’s Training
Description: Draw 2 cards. As long as Buck’s Training is next to your Active Pokémon, each of your Active Pokémon’s attacks does 10 more damage to the Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Supporter-Rule: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.

Name: Cynthia’s Feelings
Description: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw 4 cards. If any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out during your opponent’s last turn, draw 4 more cards.
Supporter-Rule: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.

Name: Energy Pickup
Description: Flip a coin. If heads, search your discard pile for a basic Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Pokémon.

Name: Poké Radar
Description: Look at the top 5 cards of your deck, choose as many Pokémon as you like, show them to your opponent, and put them into your hand. Put the other cards back on top of your deck. Shuffle your deck afterward.

Name: Snowpoint Temple
Description: Each Pokémon that isn’t an Evolved Pokémon in play (both yours and your opponent’s) gets +20 HP.
Stadium-Rule: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can’t play this card.

Name: Stark Mountain
Description: Once during each player’s turn, that player may choose a [R] or [F] Energy attached to 1 of his or her Pokémon and move that Energy to 1 of his or her Pokémon.
Stadium-Rule: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can’t play this card.

Name: Technical Machine TS-1
Effects: Search your deck for a card that evolves from 1 of your Pokémon and put it onto that Pokémon. (This counts as evolving that Pokémon.) Shuffle your deck afterward.
TM-Rule: Attach this card to 1 of your Pokémon in play. That Pokémon may use this card’s attack instead of its own.

Name: Technical Machine TS-2
Effects: Choose 1 of your opponent’s Evolved Pokémon (excluding Pokémon LV.X). Remove the highest Stage Evolution card from that Pokémon and put that card back into your opponent’s hand.
TM-Rule: Attach this card to 1 of your Pokémon in play. That Pokémon may use this card’s attack instead of its own.

Name: Claw Fossil
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: If Claw Fossil is your Active Pokémon and is damaged by an opponent’s attack (even if Claw Fossil is Knocked Out), put 1 damage counter on the Attacking Pokémon.
Description: Play Claw Fossil as if it were a [C] Basic Pokémon. (Claw Fossil counts as a Trainer card as well, but if Claw Fossil is Knocked Out, this counts as a Knocked Out Pokémon.) Claw Fossil can’t be affected by any Special Conditions and can’t retreat. At any time during your turn before your attack, you may discard Claw Fossil from play. (This doesn’t count as a Knocked Out Pokémon.)

Name: Root Fossil
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: At any time between turns, remove 1 damage counter from Root Fossil.
Description: Play Root Fossil as if it were a [C] Basic Pokémon. (Root Fossil counts as a Trainer card as well, but if Root Fossil is Knocked Out, this counts as a Knocked Out Pokémon.) Root Fossil can’t be affected by any Special Conditions and can’t retreat. At any time during your turn before your attack, you may discard Root Fossil from play. (This doesn’t count as a Knocked Out Pokémon.)

Name: Azelf LV.X
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: Each of your [P] Pokémon has no Weakness.
Effects: Choose 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. Put 1 damage counter on that Pokémon for each Energy attached to all of your opponent’s Pokémon.
Level-Up-Rule: Put this card onto your Active Azelf. Azelf LV.X can use any attack, Poké-Power, or Poké-Body from its previous level.

Name: Gliscor LV.X
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), when you put Gliscor LV.X from your hand onto your Active Gliscor, you may choose 1 of the Defending Pokémon. That Pokémon is now Paralyzed and Poisoned.
Effects: You may switch Gliscor with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Level-Up-Rule: Put this card onto your Active Gliscor. Gliscor LV.X can use any attack, Poké-Power, or Poké-Body from its previous level.

Name: Magnezone LV.X
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: As often as you like during your turn (before your attack), you may move a [L] or [M] Energy attached to 1 of your Pokémon to another of your Pokémon. This power can’t be used if Magnezone is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: Discard a [L] Energy and a [M] Energy attached to Magnezone. The Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Level-Up-Rule: Put this card onto your Active Magnezone. Magnezone LV.X can use any attack, Poké-Power, or Poké-Body from its previous level.

Name: Mesprit LV.X
Effects: Remove 3 damage counters from each of your Benched Pokémon.
If don’t have Uxie LV.X and Azelf LV.X in play, this attack does nothing. Discard all Energy attached to Mesprit.
Level-Up-Rule: Put this card onto your Active Mesprit. Mesprit LV.X can use any attack, Poké-Power, or Poké-Body from its previous level.

Name: Mewtwo LV.X
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: Prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to Mewtwo by your opponent’s Pokémon that isn’t an Evolved Pokémon.
Effects: Discard all Energy attached to Mewtwo.
Level-Up-Rule: Put this card onto your Active Mewtwo. Mewtwo LV.X can use any attack, Poké-Power, or Poké-Body from its previous level.

Name: Rhyperior LV.X
Effects: Discard the top 5 cards from your deck. This attack does 50 damage for each Energy card you discarded.
Search your discard pile for all [F] Energy cards, show them to your opponent, and shuffle them into your deck.
Level-Up-Rule: Put this card onto your Active Rhyperior. Rhyperior LV.X can use any attack, Poké-Power, or Poké-Body from its previous level.

Name: Uxie LV.X
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may look at the top 2 cards of your deck, choose 1 of them, and put it into your hand. Put the other card on the bottom of your deck. This power can’t be used if Uxie is affected by a Special Condition. You can’t use more than 1 Trade Off Poké-Power each turn.
Effects: Uxie can’t use Zen Blade during your next turn.
Level-Up-Rule: Put this card onto your Active Uxie. Uxie LV.X can use any attack, Poké-Power, or Poké-Body from its previous level.
